subject: Human Resources
title: HR in Small vs. Large Companies
description: Learn HR in Small vs. Large Companies in one minute.

hook.text: A 10-person startup has no HR department. The founder does hiring, payroll, and conflict resolution personally.

buildup.text: Small companies rely on generalists who handle everything. Large companies have specialists for each HR function.

discovery.text: At roughly 50 employees, most companies hire their first dedicated HR person—complexity demands it.

twist.text: The biggest HR mistake small companies make is waiting too long—by then, bad habits are already embedded.

climax.text: Whether one person or a department of fifty, every organization needs someone thinking about its people.

punchline.text: Company size changes HR's shape, never its importance.

quiz.question: When do most companies hire their first HR person?
